name: EULA
classification_notes: The page contains only a licensing agreement and no typeface-specific content; category fields are placeholder defaults as no typeface data is present.
summary: This page contains only Troisième Type's End User License Agreement and no typeface product information.
description: The URL provided leads to Troisième Type's licensing terms page rather than a specific typeface product page. No typeface name, designer, weights, styles or specimen information is present. A meaningful entry cannot be generated from this content alone.
foundry_blurb: Troisième Type's EULA governs desktop, web, merchandise and personal-use licenses for their fonts, with restrictions on redistribution, conversion to webfont formats, political or religious use, and NFT-related applications, operating under French intellectual property law.

name: Fonts in Use
classification_notes: This page is a gallery of client work showcasing multiple typefaces from the foundry's catalogue, not a single typeface product page. No specific typeface can be classified.
summary: A showcase page listing client and collaborator projects using Troisième Type's display typefaces across branding, print and digital work.
description: This is a 'Fonts in Use' gallery page for Troisième Type, an independent French foundry. It presents a range of real-world applications across typefaces including Anurati Pro, Blanka, Braggy, Condensein and Kassandre, used in branding, book covers, signage and video game campaigns. No single typeface is the subject of this page.
foundry_blurb: Troisième Type describes itself as an independent foundry providing original display fonts at affordable costs, with early buyers receiving free updates on purchased fonts.

name: Calvin
classification_notes: A unicase variable font with a dripping, glutinous aesthetic inspired by a sci-fi horror film; firmly display in category with no body-text utility.
summary: A variable horror display font with a dripping, glutinous aesthetic drawn from the 2017 sci-fi film Life.
description: Calvin is a unicase variable font built around four weights that stretch between a lean and a heavy, slimy register. The design takes its visual cue from the creature in the film Life (2017), giving letterforms a leaking, viscous quality suited to horror-adjacent titles and novelty graphic work. Version 1.0 covers 42 glyphs per weight with Latin script support across a broad range of languages.
foundry_blurb: Troisième Type describe Calvin as a spooky, dripping unicase variable font inspired by the 2017 movie Life, offering four glutinous weights at an affordable price point. They encourage users to try the family in the most grotesque ways possible.

name: Metropal
classification_notes: Bauhaus-inspired with sharp edges and curved figures, designed explicitly for headlines, logos and posters rather than body text, placing it firmly in the display category with geometric construction principles.
summary: A Bauhaus-inspired geometric display typeface that blends sharp edges with curved forms, offered as a variable font aimed at logos, headlines and poster work.
description: Metropal combines flat, angular construction with rounded counters in a style that draws on Bauhaus geometry. Version 1.0 ships with 50 glyphs per weight and covers a broad range of Latin-script languages including Swahili, Zulu, Indonesian and Kinyarwanda. The variable font format makes it suitable for headline and branding contexts where weight adjustment is useful, though the restricted glyph count limits its application.
foundry_blurb: Troisième Type describe Metropal as a geometric, modernised, Bauhaus-inspired variable typeface, designed by blending simple sharp edges with curved figures to achieve a clean and distinctive look. They position it for logos, branding, headlines, posters and album artwork. Version 1.0 supports Latin script with 50 glyphs per weight.

name: Anurati Pro
classification_notes: A unicase display sans-serif with geometric construction and sci-fi aesthetic, explicitly positioned as a display font best used at large sizes, placing it firmly in the display category.
summary: A unicase geometric display sans with a sci-fi inflection, offered in two weights suited to branding and large-scale editorial use.
description: Anurati Pro is a unicase display sans-serif built around geometric forms and a futuristic character. The family ships in two weights, Regular and Outline, each containing 96 glyphs with support for Latin script across a broad range of languages. It is designed to perform at large sizes, making it a natural fit for titles, logotypes and campaign headlines.
foundry_blurb: Troisième Type describe Anurati Pro as a unicase typeface shaped between geometry, sci-fi and luxury, suited to branding, web and editorial projects. They position it as a futuristic display sans-serif with a sharp personality, built to excel at large sizes, and note that version 1.0 features 96 glyphs per weight with Latin script support.

name: Braggy
classification_notes: Braggy is explicitly described as a fatty display font intended for large-scale use; its exaggerated weight and storefront-ready character place it firmly in the fat face display subcategory.
summary: A heavyweight display family with an exaggerated fat face cut and a hand-painted brush variant by signpainter Vincent Delion.
description: Built for large-scale applications such as storefronts, oversized titles and logotypes, Braggy leans into maximum weight and visual impact. The Regular style offers 293 glyphs across Basic Latin and supported European languages, while the Brush style adds 155 hand-painted glyphs in a looser, signpainting register. The two styles share a common design intention but offer distinct texture for different contexts.
foundry_blurb: Troisième Type describe Braggy as one of the boldest and fattest font families available, inspired by the visual excess of melting cheese and oversized burgers. Aimed at big storefronts, huge titles and oversized logos, version 1.0 adds a Brush style with handpainted letters by signpainter Vincent Delion, supporting Basic Latin script across 293 glyphs for the Regular style and 155 for the Brush.

name: Alezio
classification_notes: Alezio is described as a sans serif typeface with wide proportions and a modular character, consistent with a geometric sans approach suited to display and branding use.
summary: A wide, variable geometric sans with five weights and 271 alternate glyphs, covering Latin Extended and Cyrillic scripts.
description: Previously released as Jaro, Alezio is a wide-proportioned geometric sans designed for modular branding and display work. Version 0.5 ships with 687 glyphs per weight across five styles, a variable axis, and extensive alternate glyph support. Latin Extended and Cyrillic script coverage broadens its reach across Central and Eastern European languages.
foundry_blurb: Troisième Type describe Alezio as a fun variable sans serif supporting Cyrillic, previously named Jaro, and well suited to modular branding projects. Version 0.5 introduces 271 alternate glyphs and five weights, with further kerning refinements, additional characters and more alternates planned for future updates. Buyers receive free updates until version 1.0.

name: Kassandre
classification_notes: Kassandre is explicitly described as a strongly condensed sans serif, designed for display use at large sizes, inspired by the poster lettering of A.M. Cassandre.
summary: A strongly condensed display sans inspired by the iconic poster lettering of A.M. Cassandre, with discretionary ligatures for added character.
description: Currently at version 0.3 with a single Light weight, Kassandre is built around tight condensed forms drawn from the visual language of Adolphe Jean-Marie Mouron's poster work. It functions across small text and large headlines, with 411 glyphs covering Latin Extended script and a generous set of discretionary ligatures. Future updates are planned to add Thin, Medium and Bold weights, improved kerning and a variable font release.
foundry_blurb: Troisième Type describe Kassandre as a strongly condensed sans serif inspired by the tremendous and iconic work of Cassandre. They highlight its readability at small sizes, its quality at large scale and the variety of discretionary ligatures included to add uniqueness to designs. The font is offered at an early price with free updates promised through to version 1.0.

name: Candor
classification_notes: A tall, unicase display typeface with retro-modern 60s/70s influences, designed explicitly for headlines, titles and brand logos rather than body text use.
summary: A tall unicase display family of eleven weights with a stencil variant, drawing on 60s and 70s retro-modern lettering for headlines and brand marks.
description: Built around a thin, high x-height and unicase construction, Candor references the geometric display lettering of the 1960s and 70s. Eleven weights run from Thin to Black, joined by a Stencil cut and a Rune style, each containing 388 glyphs with Latin Extended coverage. Currently at version 0.3, with a rounded family and kerning refinements planned for future updates.
foundry_blurb: Troisième Type describe Candor as a tall, elegant unicase font family offering modularity, drawing inspiration from 60s and 70s retro-modern fonts to add class and quality to design. The family ships with 11 weights of 388 glyphs each and a thin, high x-height suited to headlines, titles and brand logos. Buyers at the current early version price receive free updates through to version 1.0.

name: Condensein
classification_notes: A decorative condensed display type with explicit Art Déco influences; its tall, narrow proportions and stylised forms place it firmly in the display category with an art deco subcategory.
summary: An Art Déco condensed display typeface in two styles, born from a Pink October charity collaboration and donating 30% of sales to a French breast cancer awareness association.
description: Released in version 0.1 from a late 2021 collaboration with designer Plaisant Plaisir, Condensein offers two stylised uppercase-focused styles with 385 glyphs per weight covering Latin Extended script. The typeface is presented as a work in progress, with planned updates to add lowercase letters, additional characters and refined kerning. A portion of each sale supports KEMPER'OSE, a French non-profit focused on breast and gynaecological cancer awareness.
foundry_blurb: Troisième Type describe Condensein as a stylish and feminine Art Déco condensed typeface featuring two styles, one of which is described as a little naughty. Designed collaboratively with Plaisant Plaisir for Pink October, 30% of sales go to the French non-profit KEMPER'OSE, which campaigns for awareness of breast and gynaecological cancer.

name: Modernita
classification_notes: Modernita is described as a revamp of old Italian types (Modernised Italian), featuring super-heavy slabs, hefty feet and high stroke contrast, placing it firmly in the modern slab or Italienne/Italian style tradition.
summary: A high-contrast display slab drawn from the tradition of 19th-century Italian types, currently available in Light and Black weights as an early-access release.
description: Modernita takes its name from a contraction of Modernised Italian and draws on the exaggerated stroke contrast and heavy slab serifs of the Italian display type tradition. At version 0.1 it ships with 793 glyphs per weight across Light and Black, covering Latin Extended, Cyrillic and Hebrew scripts, with lowercases and additional weights including Regular, Medium and Bold planned for future updates. The Black weight pairs super-heavy slabs and thick feet against thin hairlines for an assertive display presence.
foundry_blurb: Troisième Type describe Modernita as a revamp of old Italian types, contracting Modernised Italian into its name. Available in Light and Black at version 0.1 with 793 glyphs per weight, it supports Latin Extended, Cyrillic and Hebrew. Buyers get the current version at an early price with free updates through to version 1.0, which will add lowercases, more weights and a variable font.

name: Oldita
classification_notes: Oldita is based on an 1821 Italian typeface from Caslon & Catherwood's type specimen, a historically unusual slab-like display style with western characteristics, described by contemporaries as a typographic monstrosity, placing it firmly in the decorative slab display tradition.
summary: A variable font revival of a notoriously eccentric 1821 Italian slab typeface from Caslon & Catherwood's type specimen, currently in early release with uppercase-only character sets.
description: Drawn from a typeface that earned descriptions such as "typographic monstrosity" and "degenerate" in its own era, Oldita resurrects an 1821 display slab with a variable font axis spanning Thin to Bold. Version 0.1 ships with 478 glyphs per weight and covers Latin Extended, Cyrillic and Hebrew scripts, though lowercase characters are planned for future updates. It sits best in poster, title and display contexts where its deliberately perverse western-slab character is an asset rather than a liability.
foundry_blurb: Troisième Type describe Oldita as a take on an old Italian typeface first appearing in Caslon & Catherwood's 1821 type specimen. Historically condemned as a crude expression of perversity, it has been revisited as a variable font paying homage to this bizarre yet audacious western and slab typeface, with version 0.1 offering 478 glyphs per weight, Latin Extended, Cyrillic and Hebrew support, and free updates promised until version 1.0.

name: Ykar
classification_notes: Described as a unicase modern and futuristic sans serif, mixing rounded uppercase letterforms with a contemporary aesthetic; the unicase treatment and display intent place it firmly in the display category with geometric sans characteristics.
summary: A unicase geometric sans with rounded uppercase letterforms and a slightly short x-height, aimed at video, branding and editorial work.
description: Ykar blends contemporary rounded uppercase forms with a futuristic sensibility, presented as a unicase design that collapses the distinction between upper and lowercase. Version 1.0 ships with 91 glyphs per weight and covers a broad range of Latin-script languages. It suits projects where a clean, forward-looking identity is needed without the neutrality of a conventional grotesque.
foundry_blurb: Troisième Type describe Ykar as a unicase modern and futuristic sans serif with a slightly short x-height and a strong set of letters for everyday design use, suited to projects involving video, branding, editorial and web.

name: Blanka
classification_notes: Blanka is described as a minimalistic display font with 55 glyphs, clearly intended for large-scale use rather than text setting, placing it firmly in the display category with geometric influences.
summary: A free, open-source display font with minimalist geometry and a limited glyph set of 55 characters, suited to large-scale headlines.
description: Built on minimalist shapes, Blanka is Troisième Type's first typeface and is offered as a free, open-source download for both personal and commercial use. The font covers a basic Latin script with 55 glyphs, supporting a broad range of Latin-based languages from English and Swahili to Zulu and Cebuano. Its limited character set and single weight make it best suited to short, impactful display settings rather than extended text.
foundry_blurb: Troisième Type describe Blanka as a modern display font drawing on minimalistic shapes, noting it was the first font the foundry ever produced. It is offered as a free open-source download with 55 glyphs and Latin script support, and is recommended for large-scale typographic use.

name: Spacer36
classification_notes: A decorative all-caps display typeface with a retro-futuristic aesthetic inspired by rock band logos, exploring weight and width axes via variable font technology. Clearly display in intent with no body text application.
summary: A retro-futuristic all-caps display typeface originating from the 36 Days of Type challenge in 2022, expanded into a variable font with 12 styles exploring weight and width axes.
description: Originally drawn for the 9th edition of 36 Days of Type, Spacer36 draws on the aesthetic of rock band logos such as the Scorpions' to produce a distinctive all-caps alphabet with a space-age feel. Version 0.2 delivers a variable font with 252 glyphs per weight and Latin Extended script support across a wide range of languages. Lowercases and further character additions are planned for future updates.
foundry_blurb: Troisième Type describe Spacer36 as a retro-futuristic alphabet inspired in part by the Scorpions' logo, first drawn during the 9th edition of 36 Days of Type in 2022 before being developed further into a variable font with 12 styles across weight and width axes. Version 0.2 includes 252 glyphs per weight and Latin Extended support, with lowercases and kerning improvements to follow in future updates.

summary: This URL leads to a newsletter sign-up page for Troisième Type, not a typeface product page.

The page lists the foundry's font catalogue by name only, without any design detail, specimen imagery or technical specifications. No meaningful typeface entry can be produced from this content.

name: What's Next
classification_notes: The page provides no specimen or classification detail; listed under Troisième Type's display font catalogue as a work-in-progress entry.
summary: A forthcoming release from Troisième Type, listed as a work-in-progress with no specimen or detail currently available.
description: This entry on the Troisième Type site appears to be a placeholder for an unreleased typeface. No weights, styles or specimen images are shown, and the page content amounts to little more than the foundry's standard navigation and contact information. No classification or design intent can be assessed at this stage.
